title: A little blab.
description: A physically unsettled phase note about illness, altered self-recognition, nihilist comedy, and accepting that the current inner state cannot yet be sampled cleanly.

motifMap.meaning: The inability to take a clean reading becomes honest evidence that the current phase remains unassigned.
Identity is felt in suspension after an unnamed alteration, without forcing a replacement narrative.
Nihilist comedy offers relief while the writer watches its emotional price and possible evasiveness.

excerpt: I am in a strange phase. I feel myself and not myself. Last night something altered.

editHistory.notes: Manual live RSS extraction performed with user permission. Classified from the actual post text as The Hollow with Methodic Self-Watching because the writer explicitly inhabits a suspended identity, accepts an unclear phase reading, and observes illness and nihilist laughter without forcing coherence.
